data.body:
[G4 child-consistency — container review of parent 1f19-adf2-ffb2-4b09 paired with this child]

Reviewed the 7 cross-child interaction modes for this child against the whole sibling roster (S1 6d56, S2 70c7, S3 fa49, S5 b7fa, S6 1ba5). One finding (dependency gap); the rest are clean.

FINDING (dependency gap — snapshot handle not delivered to the plan-review claim-gate path)
- criteria: this child AC4 ("Plan-review's file_impact hashes and the claim-gate freshness re-check use the same pinned-SHA ref basis (no whole-HEAD divergence)"); parent AC1 ("all read a faithful snapshot materialized at the pinned SHA") and AC2 (signed verdict reproducible).
- location: this child (30b3) AC4 + Scope ("Out: gate parameter threading (S3)"); S3 fa49 "What"/Scope/file_impact.
- evidence:
  1. The claim-gate freshness re-check AND the signed dependency map are both computed from the SERVER WORKING TREE today: src/rebar/llm/plan_review/attest.py:106-107 (dependency_hashes hashes paths from _config.repo_root(...)) and attest.py:325-326 (the ADR-0002 freshness re-check re-hashes from _config.repo_root). Neither reads a snapshot dir.
  2. Re-rooting gate code-reading to the snapshot is S3's job, but S3 (fa49) scopes its re-rooting to the LLM file tools only ("point the gate's file tools (pai_tools root / cfg.repo_path) at the snapshot dir") and its file_impact lists completion.py / pai_tools.py / mcp_server.py — NOT plan_review/attest.py.
  3. This child's file_impact lists transition.py + completion.py — also NOT attest.py — yet AC4 (the attest.py hashing path) is in this child's "In" scope while it pushes "gate parameter threading (S3)" out of scope.
  4. No sibling in the roster lists attest.py in file_impact (confirmed by search); so the snapshot-handle/pinned-SHA hand-off INTO the plan-review attestation hashing path is owned by neither S3 nor explicitly by this child.
- scenarios: attested plan-review signs file_impact hashes taken from the snapshot at the pinned SHA, but the claim-gate freshness re-check (run later) re-hashes the same paths from the server working tree (possibly a different branch / dirty) → spurious "stale-code" drift or, worse, a re-hash that silently passes against the wrong branch — re-introducing exactly the moving-checkout false-verdict the parent epic exists to kill. AC4's "no whole-HEAD divergence" cannot be met if attest.py still resolves through _config.repo_root.
- impact: AC4 (ref-coherence) and parent AC1/AC2 (snapshot-only reads, reproducibility) are unsatisfiable as decomposed: the resolved-SHA snapshot handle is not routed into attest.py's dependency_hashes() / claim-gate re-check, and the responsibility for routing it sits in a seam between this child and S3 that neither claims.
- checklist_item: dependency gap (cross-child mode 3)
- suggested_fix: Make the snapshot-handle/pinned-SHA basis an explicit hand-off: either (a) extend S3's re-rooting scope to include src/rebar/llm/plan_review/attest.py (dependency_hashes + the ADR-0002 freshness re-check), threading the snapshot dir as the hash base in place of _config.repo_root, and list attest.py in S3's file_impact; or (b) add attest.py to THIS child's file_impact and an AC making "dependency_hashes() and the freshness re-check hash from the pinned-SHA snapshot, not _config.repo_root" explicit, since AC4 already claims claim-gate ref-coherence as in-scope here.

Clean on the other 6 modes:
- implicit shared state: verified_at_sha (resolved by S1, threaded by S3, consumed here, surfaced by S5) — consistent producer→consumer chain.
- conflicting assumptions: local-mode unsigned (S3 flags / this child bars / S5 reflects unsigned) and attested-default-still-signs (S5 AC) agree.
- scope overlap: local-mode split (S3 marks-unsigned vs this child enforces-the-bar) is clean, no double-implementation.
- ordering violation: logical S1→S3→this-child→S5 ordering; no contradiction (all tickets carry empty deps uniformly; parent AC8 only requires all-closed+green).
- consumer impact: S5 surfaces verified_at_sha; S6 documents in-toto shape + HMAC limits (this child delegates docs to S6, covered by S6 AC4).
- residual references: --force-close signal, ADR 0002, S6 all resolve.
